mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5%, it signals a labor market deterioration that triggers recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in real GDP growth and industrial production indicates a supply-demand mismatch that erodes corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict downward SPX moves in more than 55% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, rising consumer credit balances reflect resilient household spending that sustains corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon despite tight monetary policy.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when RSAFS rises while FEDFUNDS remains above 5.0%, or the cross-product term's predictive power vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reinforces earnings stability, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon as market participants price in sustained economic expansion.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in payroll growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below 1.2 million units signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and consumer confidence, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as recession risks materialize.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains elevated while unemployment ticks upward, it indicates a policy-induced labor cooling that historically precedes equity drawdowns, driving negative S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict downward SPX moves in more than 55% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of robust industrial production growth, rising consumer credit balances amplify the positive impact on corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon as earnings momentum accelerates.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when RSAFS rises while INDPRO remains above 102.0, or the cross-product term's predictive power vanishes in out-of-sample testing.
